inline_comments.message:
I will call it: **Step 9.** Verify ONAP installation

Keep the command to check to check the pods

Add a note: While all pods may be in a Running state, it is not a guarantee that all components are running fine.

Launch the healthcheck tests using Robot to verify that the components are healthy.

> command to launch the health check

shall we not detail the 3 files referenced in the command line?

onap-all.yaml lists the modules enabled in the onap deployment. As onap is very modular, it is possible to compose your onap and disable some components through this configuration file.

environment.yaml is used to adapt installation timers to the level of performance of your infrastructure.

openstack.yaml includes all the Openstack related information for the default target tenant you want to use to deploy VNFs from ONAP and/or additional parameters for the embedded tests.
